svg.title: Auto-Arch Tournament pipeline blueprint: baseline versus current champion
svg.desc: A lecture-style technical blueprint comparing the baseline RV32IM five-stage pipeline to the current optimized Auto-Arch Tournament core.
svg.text: Baseline tag: straight 5-stage RV32IM core | Current champion: hyp-20260428-054-r20s2 | Fitness gate: formal + cosim + CoreMark CRC + 3-seed FPGA PnR
pc+4 fetch, monolithic ALU, blocking memory stalls
same 5-stage skeleton, added predictors/replay, hot-path timing cuts, store-slot decoupling

g.text: - IF emits pc+4 path unless EX redirects
- No branch prediction, direct-JAL prediction, or replay
- Branch/JAL/JALR target known only in EX
- DIV/REM are in the combinational ALU cone
- Any dmem wait on a memory op freezes back to MEM
- Forwarding: EX/MEM and MEM/WB back to EX
- Single issue; RVFI channel 1 tied off
- IF prediction: backward branch + direct JAL
- IF replay: two 128-entry banks, MRU, registered candidate
- EX repair only when resolved next-PC disagrees
- DIV/REM moved to iterative side unit
- ALU hot path isolated from M-extension mux
- MEM can retire bufferable stores into one slot
- RVFI order counter segmented into 16-bit chunks
- Same top-level IO and single-issue retirement contract

BTFNT branch predictor -> direct JAL predictor -> iterative DIV/REM -> store retirement slot -> segmented RVFI counter -> registered I-fetch replay -> compressed resetless tags -> hot/cold ALU split -> banked replay predictor
